# SEV-1 INCIDENT - RELEASE BLOCKER
**Title:** Statements are filing some payments under the wrong kind of transaction, or dropping the payee name
**Owner:** Payments Operations
**Disposition:** Do not ship this release until it is resolved

## What customers reported

Over the past few days support has collected a run of tickets that all share one
shape: the money itself moved fine, but the app files the payment under a category
that does not match what the customer actually did, or shows the wrong name - or
no name at all - beside it. In every case the row that misbehaves carries one very
particular, uncommon form of raw statement text; everyday rows are labeled exactly
as before. The tickets below are anonymized, but the raw narration each customer
pasted is reproduced verbatim, because that raw text is what makes each case
reproducible.

> "I wrote a cheque against my account and the app just lists it as a general
> transfer now - nothing marks it as a cheque anymore. My earlier cheques were
> tagged correctly. The only thing sitting in that row's note is the cheque's own
> number, nothing else around it."

> "My salary came in and the amount is right, but where my employer's name should
> be the app now just prints the word `NACH`. The raw text on that row looks like
> `ACH-BD-NACH-ACME INDUSTRIES` - it used to lift the employer's name off the end
> of that reference, and now it is picking up the wrong piece of it."

> "On some transfers that come in, the sender's name is blank now. Those rows all
> share the same reference layout - a short block of letters, then a long string
> of digits, then the name - for example `GBHDL0123456789 RAVI KUMAR`. The name
> sitting at the end just is not being pulled through anymore; the name column
> comes out empty."

The same batch of complaints also covers two quieter cases that are about the
category rather than the name. The token confirmation charge a bank posts when a
new auto-debit mandate is set up - a small nominal amount, with the note flagging
it as a mandate - is being filed as an ordinary transfer instead of being
recognized as a verification entry. And a refund that came back on a card is
landing as a plain refund for some customers when it should read as a card
reversal; a customer on a different type of account sees the same kind of refund
labeled correctly, so the wrong label appears to depend on the account category.

## Scope of impact

Everyday activity - normal purchases, routine transfers, regular deposits - is
categorized and named correctly, which is precisely why this slipped through QA and
why our monitoring dashboards looked healthy. The breakage is confined to a small
set of unusual transaction forms - among them a note or reference that is exactly
a certain number of digits wide - each one hinging on some exact shape of the raw
row rather than anything about the customer, and each form individually uncommon. But they occur right across the customer base, so they
generate a steady trickle of "why is this labeled wrong" tickets and, more
damaging, they poison the spending-category summaries and payee histories that
several downstream features rely on. That combination is why we are treating it
as ship-blocking rather than a backlog item.

## What we need from engineering

The labeling path is fully deterministic and runs offline - hand it the same
transaction and it reproduces the same wrong answer every time - so each of these
is reproducible on demand and can be pinned down exactly. Get the affected forms
labeling and naming correctly again, and do it without disturbing any transaction
that is already labeled right today. A fix that rescues these cases but nudges the
behavior of ordinary, currently-correct transactions is not acceptable: the pass
condition is simply that the specific problem payments described above come out
right and everything else stays exactly where it is.

I’ll trace the deterministic statement-labeling and payee-extraction paths, identify the exact-shape branches behind all five reported cases, then add narrow regressions and verify the broader suite remains unchanged.

todos.content: Locate statement labeling, payee extraction, and relevant tests
Reproduce and isolate the five uncommon transaction regressions
Implement minimal targeted fixes with regression coverage
Run focused and full verification suites

arguments.prompt: Explore the Python codebase under /app/categorizationapp very thoroughly. Identify the precise bank scripts/functions and likely defects for these five reported forms: (1) remark/note consisting exactly of a cheque number digits should classify cheque; clues exact digit width uncommon, (2) ACH-BD-NACH-ACME INDUSTRIES should extract ACME INDUSTRIES not NACH, (3) GBHDL0123456789 RAVI KUMAR should extract trailing sender name, (4) nominal mandate verification entry should classify verification rather than transfer, (5) card refund should classify CARD_REVERSAL for one account category but plain REFUND currently. Look for suspicious narrow regex changes/typos/boundaries/order/accountCategory handling. <task id="ses_09b13e74affeac7ZIohEY5VWof" state="completed">
<task_result>
## Executive findings

Three defects are highly specific and reproducible:

1. **IDBI ACH party extraction is off by one segment**: `ACH-BD-NACH-ACME INDUSTRIES` returns `Nach`.
2. **IDBI routing-reference extraction requests a nonexistent capture group**: `GBHDL0123456789 RAVI KUMAR` returns an empty party name.
3. **IndusInd uses the inconsistent account type `SAVINGS`**: canonical `SAVING` card credits become `REFUND` instead of `CARD_PAYMENT_REVERSAL`.

The cheque and mandate reports most likely also concern **IndusInd**, although their precise fixture details matter. The existing rules are unusually narrow:

- Only a **five-digit debit** remark is treated as a cheque.
- Only a positive amount exactly equal to **2** plus `MANDATE`, or a generic positive ₹1 debit, is treated as verification.

No code anywhere defines or emits literal `CARD_REVERSAL`; the established taxonomy is `CARD_PAYMENT_REVERSAL`.

---

# 1. Digits-only cheque number in remark/note

## Strongest implicated script

**Function:** `categorize_Indusind_transactions`  
**File:** `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indusind.py`

Relevant rules:

- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indusind.py:55-57`
- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indusind.py:64`
- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indusind.py:190`

The critical rule is:

```python
transactions.remark.str.contains("^[0-9]{5}$", ...)
& transactions.type.eq("DEBIT")
```

at line 64.

## Likely defect

This is unusually narrow in two ways:

1. It accepts **exactly five digits**, while most other bank scripts accept ranges such as five or six digits.
2. It only handles `DEBIT`; there is no matching digits-only `CREDIT` rule for `CHEQUE_DEPOSIT`.

Observed behavior:

| Remark | Type | Current result |
|---|---|---|
| `12345` | `DEBIT` | `CHEQUE_PAID` |
| `12345` | `CREDIT` | `TRANSFER` |
| `123456` | `DEBIT` | `TRANSFER` |
| `123456` | `CREDIT` | `TRANSFER` |

Therefore:

- If the reported cheque number is five digits but the transaction is a credit, the missing credit counterpart is the defect.
- If it is six or another documented width, `{5}` is the defect.

There is also an apparent typo at line 57: a description-based `CHEQUE_DEPOSIT` condition is gated by `type == "DEBIT"`, where `CREDIT` is much more likely intended.

## Minimal recommended change

Use the exact width established by the fixture, not an unconstrained digits regex. Most likely:

```python
^[0-9]{5,6}$
```

with direction-specific choices:

- `DEBIT` → `CHEQUE_PAID`
- `CREDIT` → `CHEQUE_DEPOSIT`

Also verify whether line 57 should use `CREDIT`.

Do not merely remove the anchors; exact anchors are appropriate for a note that consists solely of a cheque number.

## Secondary candidates

If this form belongs to another bank, these are the notable suspicious implementations:

- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/SouthIndian.py:32`  
  Uses `[0-9]{6}` without `^...$`, so `x123456y` and seven-digit strings also match. This is over-broad, not a failure to recognize exact six digits.
- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/UnionBank.py:122-123`  
  Six- and eight-digit remarks are only used to populate `partyName`; they do not classify the transaction as a cheque. The eight-digit expression also lacks `$`.
- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indian.py:39`  
  `CLG CHQ [0-9{6}]` is a malformed character class. It means one character from `0-9`, `{`, `6`, or `}`, not six digits. This applies to the description rather than the remark.

---

# 2. `ACH-BD-NACH-ACME INDUSTRIES` should yield `ACME INDUSTRIES`

## Precise location

**Function:** `categorize_IDBI_transactions`  
**File:** `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/IDBI.py`

Relevant lines:

- Category detection: `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/IDBI.py:38`
- Party extraction: `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/IDBI.py:81-86`
- Exact defective branch: `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/IDBI.py:83`

Current branch:

```python
[
    transactions.description.str.contains("^ACH-BD-NACH", ...),
    sep_by_dash(transactions.description, 2, 3)
]
```

`sep_by_dash` is defined at:

- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/AppUtil.py:17-18`

It splits on `-` and selects the zero-based slice.

For:

```text
ACH-BD-NACH-ACME INDUSTRIES
```

the parts are:

```text
0 ACH
1 BD
2 NACH
3 ACME INDUSTRIES
```

The code selects part 2, so current output is `Nach`.

## Minimal recommended change

For the specific `^ACH-BD-NACH` branch, select segment 3:

```python
sep_by_dash(transactions.description, 3, 4)
```

Keep this branch before the generic `^ACH-BD-` branch at line 84. The order is important because `np.select` uses the first matching condition.

## Reproduced current result

With a debit row:

```text
transactionCategory: AUTO_PAYMENT
partyName: Nach
```

Expected:

```text
transactionCategory: AUTO_PAYMENT
partyName: Acme Industries
```

---

# 3. `GBHDL0123456789 RAVI KUMAR` should yield trailing sender

## Precise location

**Function:** `categorize_IDBI_transactions`  
**File:** `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/IDBI.py`

Defective line:

- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/IDBI.py:110`

Current code:

```python
[
    transactions.description.str.contains(
        "^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{10,}", ...
    ),
    py_extract(
        transactions.description,
        pat="[0-9]{10,} (.*)",
        index=1
    )
]
```

`py_extract` is defined at:

- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/AppUtil.py:61-66`

It calls `Series.str.extract(...).get(index)`.

## Precise defect

The extraction regex contains only **one capture group**:

```regex
(.*)
```

That group is column/index `0`. The code requests `index=1`, which does not exist. `py_extract` therefore returns an empty series.

This is not a failure of the five-letter/ten-digit detection pattern; that detector correctly matches `GBHDL0123456789`.

## Minimal recommended change

Change only:

```python
index=1
```

to:

```python
index=0
```

Optionally make the extractor as specific as its guard:

```regex
^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{10,}\s+(.+)$
```

but the capture-index correction is the smallest safe repair.

## Reproduced current result

```text
transactionCategory: TRANSFER
partyName: ""
```

Expected party name after title-casing:

```text
Ravi Kumar
```

The transaction category can remain `TRANSFER`; this report concerns sender extraction.

---

# 4. Nominal mandate verification classified as transfer

## Most likely script

**Function:** `categorize_Indusind_transactions`  
**File:** `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indusind.py`

Relevant lines:

- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indusind.py:29`
- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indusind.py:30`
- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indusind.py:31`
- Default at `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indusind.py:79-80`

Current special-case rule:

```python
transactions.amount.eq(2)
& transactions.description.str.contains(
    "MANDATE|Mandate",
    na=False,
    case=False
)
```

→ `ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION`

## Important qualification

A positive `amount == 2` description containing `MANDATE` already classifies correctly, regardless of debit/credit. Thus, if the exact fixture has those values, the defect is not reproducible in the bank function.

The narrow cases that currently fall through to `TRANSFER` include:

- `MANDATE`, amount `1`, type `CREDIT`
- `MANDATE`, amount `-1`
- `MANDATE`, amount `-2`
- Any other nominal amount such as `0.01`
- Abbreviations such as `MNDT` unless another verification expression happens to match

A plain positive ₹1 debit is classified as verification by line 31, but that rule does not require any verification wording and does not cover credit or signed-negative representations.

## Likely defect

The special case is overly tied to one exact positive amount and one full spelling. If the reported amount is represented as signed debit/credit, `.eq(2)` excludes `-2`. If the amount is ₹1, only debit is covered.

## Minimal recommended change

First confirm the reported fixture’s exact `amount`, `type`, and description. Then make the smallest corresponding adjustment, for example:

```python
transactions.amount.abs().eq(2)
```

if the only discrepancy is signed amount, or:

```python
transactions.amount.abs().isin([1, 2])
& transactions.description.str.contains(r"\bMANDATE\b|\bMNDT\b", ...)
```

if both nominal values are valid verification forms.

Keep this verification condition before auto-mandate charge and transfer/default rules.

## Axis alternative

Axis has a parallel narrow rule:

- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Axis.py:24-26`

At line 25 it only accepts positive `amount == 1` with `MANDATE`. A signed `-1` or a ₹2 mandate falls through unless another verification expression matches. If the reported bank is Axis rather than IndusInd, the same absolute-value issue applies there.

---

# 5. Card refund should be card reversal for one account category

## Precise location and defect

**Function:** `categorize_Indusind_transactions`  
**File:** `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indusind.py`

Relevant lines:

- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/Indusind.py:26-27`

Current rules:

```python
# line 26
... amount.ge(10) & (accountType=="SAVINGS")
→ "CARD_PAYMENT_REVERSAL"

# line 27
... & (accountType=="SAVING")
→ "REFUND"
```

The controller and the rest of the codebase consistently use canonical:

```text
SAVING
```

not `SAVINGS`, for example:

- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/Controller.py:466`
- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/ICICI.py:105`
- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/BankScripts/HDFC.py:124`

## Reproduced current behavior

For a credit such as:

```text
description = "POS TXN ACME"
amount = 100
type = "CREDIT"
```

results are:

| `acc_type` | Current category |
|---|---|
| `SAVING` | `REFUND` |
| `SAVINGS` | `CARD_PAYMENT_REVERSAL` |
| `CURRENT` | `TRANSFER` |

This is a clear `SAVINGS`/`SAVING` typo. Because `np.select` uses first-match ordering, correcting line 26 to `SAVING` causes qualifying credits to hit the reversal rule before the broader refund rule.

## Minimal recommended change

Change only:

```python
accountType == "SAVINGS"
```

to:

```python
accountType == "SAVING"
```

Retain line 26 before line 27.

## Taxonomy warning

There is no `CARD_REVERSAL` category anywhere in the repository.

The established category is:

```text
CARD_PAYMENT_REVERSAL
```

defined at:

- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/TransactionCategoryEnums.py:94`

Other bank scripts also emit `CARD_PAYMENT_REVERSAL`. Unless an external contract explicitly requires a new literal, the expected output should be `CARD_PAYMENT_REVERSAL`, not `CARD_REVERSAL`.

---

# Invocation and required schema

## HTTP endpoint

Defined at:

- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/Controller.py:69-87`

Endpoint:

```http
POST /categorizeTransaction
Content-Type: application/json
```

Top-level payload keys:

```json
{
  "bank": "IDBI",
  "acc_type": "SAVING",
  "accountUID": "test-account",
  "accountHolder": "",
  "isPFMEnabled": false,
  "transactions": [
    {
      "description": "ACH-BD-NACH-ACME INDUSTRIES",
      "amount": 100,
      "type": "DEBIT",
      "remark": "",
      "balanceAfterTransaction": 1000
    }
  ]
}
```

Relevant bank selectors:

- `IDBI`: `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/Controller.py:235-258`
- `AXIS`: `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/Controller.py:427-450`
- `INDUSIND`: `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/Controller.py:451-474`

The payload calls the account category `acc_type`, while bank functions receive it as `accountType`.

## Minimum practical transaction columns

For direct invocation of the implicated bank functions, provide:

- `description`: string
- `amount`: numeric
- `type`: normally `"DEBIT"` or `"CREDIT"`
- `remark`: string, including `""` when absent
- `balanceAfterTransaction`: numeric

`clean_input_df` converts `amount` and `balanceAfterTransaction` to numeric and strips `description`:

- `/app/categorizationapp/categorizationapp/UtilityScripts/AppUtil.py:6-12`

Direct example:

```python
df = pd.DataFrame([{
    "description": "GBHDL0123456789 RAVI KUMAR",
    "amount": 100,
    "type": "CREDIT",
    "remark": "",
    "balanceAfterTransaction": 1000
}])

result = categorize_IDBI_transactions(df, "SAVING")
```

---

# Recommended minimal patch set

Subject to confirming the cheque width and mandate amount:

1. **IDBI line 83:** change dash slice `2,3` → `3,4`.
2. **IDBI line 110:** change `py_extract(..., index=1)` → `index=0`.
3. **IndusInd line 26:** change `SAVINGS` → `SAVING`.
4. **IndusInd cheque rule:** expand only to the documented exact width and add the missing credit counterpart; also inspect line 57’s `DEBIT` gate.
5. **IndusInd/Axis mandate rule:** normalize signed nominal amounts with `.abs()` and include only the documented nominal values/spellings.

No files were edited.

+import unittest
+
+import pandas as pd
+
+from categorizationapp.BankScripts.IDBI import categorize_IDBI_transactions
+from categorizationapp.BankScripts.Indusind import categorize_Indusind_transactions
+
+
+def transaction(description, amount, txn_type, remark=""):
+    return pd.DataFrame(
+        [
+            {
+                "description": description,
+                "amount": amount,
+                "type": txn_type,
+                "remark": remark,
+                "balanceAfterTransaction": 1000,
+            }
+        ]
+    )
+
+
+class StatementRegressionTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_idbi_ach_bd_nach_uses_trailing_party_name(self):
+        result = categorize_IDBI_transactions(
+            transaction("ACH-BD-NACH-ACME INDUSTRIES", 100, "CREDIT"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "partyName"], "Acme Industries")
+
+    def test_idbi_routing_reference_uses_trailing_party_name(self):
+        result = categorize_IDBI_transactions(
+            transaction("GBHDL0123456789 RAVI KUMAR", 100, "CREDIT"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "partyName"], "Ravi Kumar")
+
+    def test_indusind_six_digit_remark_is_a_paid_cheque(self):
+        result = categorize_Indusind_transactions(
+            transaction("PAYMENT", 100, "DEBIT", remark="123456"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "transactionCategory"], "CHEQUE_PAID")
+
+    def test_indusind_longer_numeric_remark_remains_a_transfer(self):
+        result = categorize_Indusind_transactions(
+            transaction("PAYMENT", 100, "DEBIT", remark="1234567"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "transactionCategory"], "TRANSFER")
+
+    def test_indusind_one_unit_mandate_entry_is_account_verification(self):
+        result = categorize_Indusind_transactions(
+            transaction("MANDATE REGISTRATION", 1, "CREDIT"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "transactionCategory"], "ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION")
+
+    def test_indusind_non_nominal_mandate_entry_remains_a_transfer(self):
+        result = categorize_Indusind_transactions(
+            transaction("MANDATE REGISTRATION", 3, "CREDIT"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "transactionCategory"], "TRANSFER")
+
+    def test_indusind_saving_card_credit_is_a_card_reversal(self):
+        result = categorize_Indusind_transactions(
+            transaction("POS TXN ACME", 100, "CREDIT"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "transactionCategory"], "CARD_PAYMENT_REVERSAL")
+
+    def test_indusind_current_card_credit_is_not_reclassified(self):
+        result = categorize_Indusind_transactions(
+            transaction("POS TXN ACME", 100, "CREDIT"), "CURRENT"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "transactionCategory"], "TRANSFER")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":

+import unittest
+
+import pandas as pd
+
+from categorizationapp.BankScripts.IDBI import categorize_IDBI_transactions
+from categorizationapp.BankScripts.Indusind import categorize_Indusind_transactions
+
+
+def transaction(description, amount, txn_type, remark=""):
+    return pd.DataFrame(
+        [
+            {
+                "description": description,
+                "amount": amount,
+                "type": txn_type,
+                "remark": remark,
+                "balanceAfterTransaction": 1000,
+            }
+        ]
+    )
+
+
+class StatementRegressionTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_idbi_ach_bd_nach_uses_trailing_party_name(self):
+        result = categorize_IDBI_transactions(
+            transaction("ACH-BD-NACH-ACME INDUSTRIES", 100, "CREDIT"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "partyName"], "Acme Industries")
+
+    def test_idbi_routing_reference_uses_trailing_party_name(self):
+        result = categorize_IDBI_transactions(
+            transaction("GBHDL0123456789 RAVI KUMAR", 100, "CREDIT"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "partyName"], "Ravi Kumar")
+
+    def test_indusind_six_digit_remark_is_a_paid_cheque(self):
+        result = categorize_Indusind_transactions(
+            transaction("PAYMENT", 100, "DEBIT", remark="123456"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "transactionCategory"], "CHEQUE_PAID")
+
+    def test_indusind_longer_numeric_remark_remains_a_transfer(self):
+        result = categorize_Indusind_transactions(
+            transaction("PAYMENT", 100, "DEBIT", remark="1234567"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "transactionCategory"], "TRANSFER")
+
+    def test_indusind_one_unit_mandate_entry_is_account_verification(self):
+        result = categorize_Indusind_transactions(
+            transaction("MANDATE REGISTRATION", 1, "CREDIT"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "transactionCategory"], "ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION")
+
+    def test_indusind_non_nominal_mandate_entry_remains_a_transfer(self):
+        result = categorize_Indusind_transactions(
+            transaction("MANDATE REGISTRATION", 3, "CREDIT"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "transactionCategory"], "TRANSFER")
+
+    def test_indusind_saving_card_credit_is_a_card_reversal(self):
+        result = categorize_Indusind_transactions(
+            transaction("POS TXN ACME", 100, "CREDIT"), "SAVING"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "transactionCategory"], "CARD_PAYMENT_REVERSAL")
+
+    def test_indusind_current_card_credit_is_not_reclassified(self):
+        result = categorize_Indusind_transactions(
+            transaction("POS TXN ACME", 100, "CREDIT"), "CURRENT"
+        )
+
+        self.assertEqual(result.at[0, "transactionCategory"], "TRANSFER")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()
